title: Baccharis pseudotenuifolia
taxonomy: Baccharis pseudotenuifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to South America and belongs to the genus Baccharis. The genus Baccharis is part of the tribe Astereae within the family Asteraceae. Baccharis pseudotenuifolia is closely related to other Baccharis species, which are widely distributed in the Americas. Baccharis pseudotenuifolia is a member of the large and diverse family of daisy-like plants, Asteraceae.

Baccharis pseudotenuifolia has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. intro: Baccharis pseudotenuifolia, also known as "palo amarillo," is a species of flowering plant in the aster family, native to South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help in the treatment of various conditions, including wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Baccharis pseudotenuifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Baccharis pseudotenuifolia is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, often used to treat respiratory problems and skin conditions.
